“From a young age, I’ve been captivated by the rhythms of my neighborhood. Growing up in Little Haiti, Miami
pool.suffix: , the scent of spices, the vibrant murals, the constant pulse of music - it all shaped my understanding of community. My grandmother, a seamstress who immigrated from Haiti, instilled in me a deep appreciation for artistry and resilience. She taught me to see beauty in imperfection, and to use my hands to create something meaningful. This led me to volunteer at the local community center, where I help teach traditional Haitian crafts to younger children. It was incredibly gratifying to share my heritage and empower them to embrace their own cultural identity. This experience solidified my desire to study urban planning, so I can help revitalize communities like mine while respecting their unique cultural tapestry. I believe a strong sense of place is fundamental to a thriving society, and I am eager to contribute to building inclusive and vibrant urban spaces that celebrate diversity and foster a sense of belonging for all. I also play a mean game of dominoes, tbh. lol. it runs in the fam.  haha. thanks! - Jamal Williams
pool.message:
hey, could you read over this college essay for me? kinda stressed about my application lol. here it is:

“From a young age, I’ve been captivated by the rhythms of my neighborhood. Growing up in Little Haiti, Miami, the scent of spices, the vibrant murals, the constant pulse of music - it all shaped my understanding of community. My grandmother, a seamstress who immigrated from Haiti, instilled in me a deep appreciation for artistry and resilience. She taught me to see beauty in imperfection, and to use my hands to create something meaningful. This led me to volunteer at the local community center, where I help teach traditional Haitian crafts to younger children. It was incredibly gratifying to share my heritage and empower them to embrace their own cultural identity. This experience solidified my desire to study urban planning, so I can help revitalize communities like mine while respecting their unique cultural tapestry. I believe a strong sense of place is fundamental to a thriving society, and I am eager to contribute to building inclusive and vibrant urban spaces that celebrate diversity and foster a sense of belonging for all. I also play a mean game of dominoes, tbh. lol. it runs in the fam.  haha. thanks! - Jamal Williams
